result.content: FINAL_ANSWER: Implement a Bayesian active learning framework: 1. Spatial Prior: Initialize P(c) = 1/N * sum_{i,p} I(c in polyomino_i at position p), where N is the total number of valid placements. 2. Likelihood Model: For an aggregate query Q on a set of cells S, use P(Q|S) = Binomial(Q; |S_oil|, p_noise), where S_oil is the true number of oil cells in S. 3. EIG Selection: Select the next probe S to maximize Expected Information Gain (EIG): EIG(S) = H(P) - E[H(P|Q)], where H(P) = -sum P(c)log P(c) is the current entropy. 4. Refinement: Maintain a set of consistent placements M = {m | m matches all drilling and aggregate query results}. 5. Final Guess: After the budget is exhausted, select cells C = {c | (sum_{m in M} I(c in m))/|M| > threshold}. JUSTIFICATION: This strategy minimizes probing cost by using information theory (Entropy/EIG) to select queries that most significantly reduce uncertainty about the polyomino placements, while the Bayesian update handles the noise in aggregate queries.
